title: AI is making workplace coaching accessible to everyone
gist: A short WEF video blurb in which BetterUp CEO Alexi Robichaux argues AI coaching is broadening access to mentoring so employees at every organizational level can get guidance. NOTE: thin video-page text, so a light card.

insights.claim: BetterUp CEO Alexi Robichaux argues AI coaching is on the rise and democratizes mentoring, extending guidance previously reserved for senior staff to people at every level of an organization.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: AI-delivered coaching turns a high-touch executive perk into a scalable per-seat SaaS market; watch HR-tech incumbents and BetterUp-style platforms for margin expansion as marginal coaching cost falls.
insights.quote: AI coaching is on the rise, broadening access to mentoring at work, so that people at every level of an organization can receive guidance
insights.locator: AI is making workplace coaching accessible to everyone

insights.claim: WEF frames the central commercialization challenge as scaling rather than inventing — arguing that despite booming AI, accelerating quantum and billions into biotech, the world isn't yet feeling the impact because turning breakthroughs into real-world progress is the hard part.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Reinforces a deployment-gap thesis: returns accrue to whoever solves scaling and adoption, not raw R&D; a lens for separating hype-stage from impact-stage tech bets.
insights.quote: Innovation is only the beginning – the real challenge is scaling what works.
insights.locator: AI is booming. Quantum breakthroughs are accelerating.
